title: Compliance Risk Assessment: AI-Based Loan Approval and Credit Risk Assessment System
use_case_summary:
**AI System:** AI-Based Loan Approval and Credit Risk Assessment System
**Description:** This AI system is used by financial institutions to evaluate loan applications and assess the creditworthiness of applicants. It analyzes structured data (e.g., income, credit history, employment status) and behavioral data (e.g., spending patterns, transaction history) to predict the likelihood of loan repayment.

The system uses machine learning models trained on historical loan data to generate a risk score for each applicant. Based on this score, the system can recommend approval, rejection, or further manual review. The goal is to improve decision speed, reduce human bias, and enhance risk managemen
**Domain:** Finance / Banking
**Users Affected:** Loan applicants, bank employees, financial institutions
**Personal Data:** Yes
**Sensitive Data:** Yes
**Automated Decisions:** Yes
**Impacts Rights:** Yes
explanation: This AI system (AI-Based Loan Approval and Credit Risk Assessment System) has been classified as High Risk. Operating in the Finance / Banking domain. It processes personal data, triggering GDPR obligations. It may impact fundamental rights, opportunities, or legal status of individuals. Relevant regulatory references: EU_AI_ACT_2024.pdf (Full Document); eu_ai_act_excerpts.txt (Article 10 - Data and data governance); EU_AI_ACT_2024.pdf (Full Document). Compliance concerns identified: Privacy Concern, Fairness / Bias Concern, Human Oversight Concern, Transparency Concern. Classification confidence: 72%

Article 9 - Risk management system
1. A risk management system shall be established, implemented, documented and maintained in relation to high-risk AI systems.
2. The risk management system shall be understood as a continuous iterative process planned and run throughout the entire lifecycle of a high-risk AI system, requiring regular systematic updating.

recommended_mitigations: Document the lawful basis for processing personal data under GDPR Article 6
Conduct a Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) per GDPR Article 35
Implement human oversight mechanisms and appeal pathways
Conduct a fundamental rights impact assessment
Perform a bias and fairness assessment on training data and model outputs
disclaimer: This is a compliance risk assessment for decision-support purposes only. It does not constitute legal advice.
